channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Fighting between the U.S. and Iran appears to be on hold -- for now
item.description: The exchange of fire between the U.S. and Iran appeared to have stopped overnight Thursday as Iran buried its Supreme Leader.
item.content:encoded: The exchange of fire between the U.S. and Iran appeared to have stopped overnight Thursday as Iran buried its Supreme Leader.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn

item.title: Retired general on whether Iran has damaged U.S. military assets in the Gulf region
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with retired four-star general Joseph Votel about U.S. bases in the Middle East in the midst of the Iran War.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with retired four-star general Joseph Votel about U.S. bases in the Middle East in the midst of the Iran War.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: He wished 'he hadn't done it,' roommate of Charlie Kirk's accused killer says
item.description: Prosecutors allege that Tyler Robinson, the suspect in Charlie Kirk's assassination, admitted to the murder in messages read aloud to the courtroom Thursday.
item.content:encoded: Prosecutors allege that Tyler Robinson, the suspect in Charlie Kirk's assassination, admitted to the murder in messages read aloud to the courtroom Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Sean Higgins

item.title: Trump leans on 'communist' messaging as economic angst drives Democrats
item.description: During the last election, when struggling to find a memorable attack line against Kamala Harris, Trump eventually landed on "Comrade Kamala."
item.content:encoded:
During the last election, when struggling to find a memorable attack line against Kamala Harris, Trump eventually landed on "Comrade Kamala."

(Image credit: Chris McGrath)
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Fighting between U.S. and Iran appears to have stopped as Iran buried its Supreme Leader, TPS holders close to losing work permits, investigation continues into Mississippi teen's death.
item.content:encoded: Fighting between U.S. and Iran appears to have stopped as Iran buried its Supreme Leader, TPS holders close to losing work permits, investigation continues into Mississippi teen's death.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: A federal panel advances Trump's proposed arch, despite unanswered questions
item.description: The National Capital Planning Commission greenlit preliminary site and building plans after hours of public pushback. It wants more details before what could be its final review in September.
item.content:encoded: The National Capital Planning Commission greenlit preliminary site and building plans after hours of public pushback. It wants more details before what could be its final review in September.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: The Rolling Stones' new album, 'Foreign Tongues,' makes the case to keep rocking
item.description: With the release of the Rolling Stones' latest studio effort, "Foreign Tongues," NPR Music's Ann Powers reviews that album and makes the case for Mick and Keith to keep going as long as they want.
item.content:encoded: With the release of the Rolling Stones' latest studio effort, "Foreign Tongues," NPR Music's Ann Powers reviews that album and makes the case for Mick and Keith to keep going as long as they want.
item.dc:creator: Ann Powers

item.title: For Mitch McConnell and Congress, health transparency is a choice, not a requirement
item.description: It's been nearly four weeks since Sen. Mitch McConnell, R-Ky., was hospitalized. It's not the first time this year that a member of Congress has been absent for weeks with scant details.
item.content:encoded:
It's been nearly four weeks since Sen. Mitch McConnell, R-Ky., was hospitalized. It's not the first time this year that a member of Congress has been absent for weeks with scant details.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: Some Republican states push for new college accreditation agency
item.description: Some Republican states are starting a new college accreditation agency to increase what they call "intellectual diversity".
item.content:encoded: Some Republican states are starting a new college accreditation agency to increase what they call "intellectual diversity".
item.dc:creator: Orlando Montoya

item.title: Field narrows at the World Cup as the quarterfinals continue
item.description: The quarterfinals at the World Cup continue Friday with 2010 champions Spain taking on Belgium. Two more matches follow on Saturday.
item.content:encoded: The quarterfinals at the World Cup continue Friday with 2010 champions Spain taking on Belgium. Two more matches follow on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
